title: [LLHD] Assigning array elements individually creates a combinational loop
body:
The Mem2Reg pass converts array element assignments into `hw.array_inject`s. But there is no canonicalizer that detects if a chain of injects fully specify the array, such that they could be turned into a `hw.array_create`. This leads to combinational loops, since the first array inject takes the current value of the array, updates it, until the last array inject is then used as the new value for the signal. This doesn't happen for `hw.struct_inject`, which has the necessary canonicalizers.

Consider this input as an example:
```mlir
hw.module @FullyDefinedArray(in %a : i5, in %b : i5, in %c : i5, out z : !hw.array<3xi5>) {
  %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
  %c1_i2 = hw.constant 1 : i2
  %c-2_i2 = hw.constant -2 : i2
  %c0_i15 = hw.constant 0 : i15
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %1 = hw.bitcast %c0_i15 : (i15) -> !hw.array<3xi5>
  %array = llhd.sig %1 : !hw.array<3xi5>
  %2 = llhd.prb %array : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // 2 preds: ^bb0, ^bb1
    %3 = llhd.sig.array_get %array[%c0_i2] : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
    %4 = llhd.sig.array_get %array[%c1_i2] : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
    %5 = llhd.sig.array_get %array[%c-2_i2] : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
    llhd.drv %3, %a after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %4, %b after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %5, %c after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.wait (%2, %a, %b, %c : !hw.array<3xi5>, i5, i5, i5), ^bb1
  }
  hw.output %2 : !hw.array<3xi5>
}

hw.module @FullyDefinedStruct(in %a : i4, in %b : i5, in %c : i6, out z : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>) {
  %c0_i15 = hw.constant 0 : i15
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %1 = hw.bitcast %c0_i15 : (i15) -> !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
  %struct = llhd.sig %1 : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
  %2 = llhd.prb %struct : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // 2 preds: ^bb0, ^bb1
    %3 = llhd.sig.struct_extract %struct["u"] : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
    %4 = llhd.sig.struct_extract %struct["v"] : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
    %5 = llhd.sig.struct_extract %struct["w"] : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
    llhd.drv %3, %a after %0 : !hw.inout<i4>
    llhd.drv %4, %b after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %5, %c after %0 : !hw.inout<i6>
    llhd.wait (%2, %a, %b, %c : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>, i4, i5, i6), ^bb1
  }
  hw.output %2 : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
}
```

Running this through `circt-verilog` produces the following:
```mlir
hw.module @FullyDefinedArray(in %a : i5, in %b : i5, in %c : i5, out z : !hw.array<3xi5>) {
  %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
  %c1_i2 = hw.constant 1 : i2
  %c-2_i2 = hw.constant -2 : i2
  %0 = hw.array_inject %2[%c0_i2], %a : !hw.array<3xi5>, i2
  %1 = hw.array_inject %0[%c1_i2], %b : !hw.array<3xi5>, i2
  %2 = hw.array_inject %1[%c-2_i2], %c : !hw.array<3xi5>, i2
  hw.output %2 : !hw.array<3xi5>
}

hw.module @FullyDefinedStruct(in %a : i4, in %b : i5, in %c : i6, out z : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>) {
  %0 = hw.struct_create (%a, %b, %c) : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
  hw.output %0 : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
}
```
Notice how the array inject ops form a loop. This is a false loop however, since the injects assign every field of the array, such that the initial value of the array does not propagate through the chain. The struct case further down has a canonicalizer that works around this issue.
code_blocks:
hw.module @FullyDefinedArray(in %a : i5, in %b : i5, in %c : i5, out z : !hw.array<3xi5>) {
  %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
  %c1_i2 = hw.constant 1 : i2
  %c-2_i2 = hw.constant -2 : i2
  %c0_i15 = hw.constant 0 : i15
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %1 = hw.bitcast %c0_i15 : (i15) -> !hw.array<3xi5>
  %array = llhd.sig %1 : !hw.array<3xi5>
  %2 = llhd.prb %array : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // 2 preds: ^bb0, ^bb1
    %3 = llhd.sig.array_get %array[%c0_i2] : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
    %4 = llhd.sig.array_get %array[%c1_i2] : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
    %5 = llhd.sig.array_get %array[%c-2_i2] : !hw.inout<array<3xi5>>
    llhd.drv %3, %a after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %4, %b after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %5, %c after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.wait (%2, %a, %b, %c : !hw.array<3xi5>, i5, i5, i5), ^bb1
  }
  hw.output %2 : !hw.array<3xi5>
}

hw.module @FullyDefinedStruct(in %a : i4, in %b : i5, in %c : i6, out z : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>) {
  %c0_i15 = hw.constant 0 : i15
  %0 = llhd.constant_time <0ns, 0d, 1e>
  %1 = hw.bitcast %c0_i15 : (i15) -> !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
  %struct = llhd.sig %1 : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
  %2 = llhd.prb %struct : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
  llhd.process {
    cf.br ^bb1
  ^bb1:  // 2 preds: ^bb0, ^bb1
    %3 = llhd.sig.struct_extract %struct["u"] : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
    %4 = llhd.sig.struct_extract %struct["v"] : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
    %5 = llhd.sig.struct_extract %struct["w"] : !hw.inout<struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>>
    llhd.drv %3, %a after %0 : !hw.inout<i4>
    llhd.drv %4, %b after %0 : !hw.inout<i5>
    llhd.drv %5, %c after %0 : !hw.inout<i6>
    llhd.wait (%2, %a, %b, %c : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>, i4, i5, i6), ^bb1
  }
  hw.output %2 : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
}
hw.module @FullyDefinedArray(in %a : i5, in %b : i5, in %c : i5, out z : !hw.array<3xi5>) {
  %c0_i2 = hw.constant 0 : i2
  %c1_i2 = hw.constant 1 : i2
  %c-2_i2 = hw.constant -2 : i2
  %0 = hw.array_inject %2[%c0_i2], %a : !hw.array<3xi5>, i2
  %1 = hw.array_inject %0[%c1_i2], %b : !hw.array<3xi5>, i2
  %2 = hw.array_inject %1[%c-2_i2], %c : !hw.array<3xi5>, i2
  hw.output %2 : !hw.array<3xi5>
}

hw.module @FullyDefinedStruct(in %a : i4, in %b : i5, in %c : i6, out z : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>) {
  %0 = hw.struct_create (%a, %b, %c) : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
  hw.output %0 : !hw.struct<u: i4, v: i5, w: i6>
}
